about: Nearly every game position in Hikaru no Go is a real professional game — the manga's go content was supervised by professional player Yukari Umezawa (5-dan), and fans spent years hunting down the original kifu. Sai's games are mostly by Honinbo Shusaku, the historical master whose ghost Sai is. This pack maps landmark manga moments to the actual game records, identified by the go community (see data/SOURCES.md).
source: Mapping: Sensei's Library (HikaruNoGo/Games). Game records: Andries Brouwer's database (CWI).

games.manga: Chapter 1 — Sai's game at the Heian court
games.title: Honinbo Dosaku vs Yasui Chitetsu
games.trivia: The game where Sai's rival cheats is actually by Dosaku — considered one of the two greatest players ever, and a fitting stand-in for a go instructor to the emperor.

games.manga: Chapter 2 — Toya Meijin's game on TV
games.title: Otake Hideo vs Takemiya Masaki, 18th Meijin League
games.trivia: The game Hikaru glimpses on TV at go class. Takemiya is famous for his 'cosmic style' aiming at grand center territory — try the area painting overlay on this one.

games.manga: Chapter 2 — Hikaru (Sai) plays Akira for the first time
games.title: Honinbo Shusaku vs Honinbo Shuwa
games.trivia: The game that starts the great rivalry is real Shusaku — Sai plays his own historical game. It also appears as game 8 in the classic book 'Appreciating Famous Games'.

games.manga: Chapter 8 — Hikaru vs Kaga at the school festival
games.title: Karigane Junichi vs Honinbo Shusai, the 1926 'killing game'
games.trivia: The famous Nihon Ki-in vs Kiseisha showdown: a brutal killing race that Karigane lost on time (W+T) — one of very few top games ever decided by the clock.

games.manga: Bonus chapter after 51 — the triple ko
games.title: Nagano Kaizen vs Inoue Shunseki
games.trivia: The first confirmed triple ko in Japanese go history — the game was declared void, exactly as retold in the manga. Triple ko is traditionally considered an ill omen.

games.title: Honinbo Shusaku vs Nakagawa Junsetsu
games.trivia: When Sai gets to play seriously, the manga reaches for a game where Shusaku crushes his opponent by 15 points — no komi, all skill.

games.title: Rin Kaiho vs Ishida Yoshio, 12th Meijin, game 1
games.trivia: A rarity: this real game ended in jigo — a perfect draw, W+0. Ishida 'Computer' Yoshio was renowned for exactly this kind of counting precision.

games.title: Yasuda (Honinbo) Shusaku vs Honinbo Shuwa
games.trivia: The moment Hikaru starts finding moves by channeling Sai is scored with young Shusaku beating his own future teacher Shuwa.

games.manga: Chapters 90–91 — Hikaru vs Waya, pro exam round 26
games.title: Cho Chikun vs Otake Hideo, 5th Meijin title, game 2
games.trivia: From the legendary 1980 Meijin match that made 24-year-old Cho Chikun the youngest Meijin ever at the time. Cho also authored the tsumego collection bundled in this app.

games.manga: Chapters 94–95 — Hikaru vs Ochi, final pro exam round
games.title: Ebizawa Kenzo vs Honinbo Shusaku
games.trivia: Hikaru's make-or-break exam game is Shusaku playing White — and the same record returns in chapter 127 for the taxi-driver Kawai's big game.

games.manga: Chapters 100–101 — Sai vs Toya Meijin ('1 dan vs 9 dan')
games.title: Cho Chikun vs Otake Hideo, 44th Oza preliminaries
games.trivia: The same two rivals as chapters 90–91, sixteen years later — the manga quietly reused go history's longest-running rivalry twice.

games.manga: Chapters 112–116 — Sai vs Toya Meijin, the internet game
games.title: Rin Kaiho vs Yoda Norimoto, 22nd Meijin League
games.trivia: The climactic Sai vs Toya Meijin game — decided by half a point in real life too. Hikaru's flash of insight about a move that could have turned the game is the heart of chapter 116.

games.title: Kobayashi Satoru vs Cho Chikun, 17th NEC Cup final
games.trivia: The board shown just before Sai disappears. In reality it was played on Valentine's Day 1998 — the manga turned it into one of the saddest scenes in go fiction.

games.manga: Chapters 145–146 — Hikaru vs Akira, official game
games.title: Honda Kunihisa vs Nakano Hironari, 47th NHK Cup
games.trivia: The rivals' first official pro clash — the moment Akira finally sees Sai inside Hikaru's go.

games.manga: Chapters 184–189 — Hikaru vs Ko Yongha, Hokuto Cup
games.title: Yoo Changhyuk vs Lee Sedol, 15th Fujitsu Cup final
games.trivia: The manga's finale runs on a half-point Fujitsu Cup final won by a young Lee Sedol — the same Lee Sedol who would face AlphaGo fourteen years later (that match is in the Famous Games pack).
